Chapter 5 — Evaluation Criteria Before O — What was missing was not in the output. It was never in the prompt. She had not told Claude — or herself — how she would

S02.meta: // SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · State acceptance criteria in a prompt so Claude knows what the output must satisfy
Include failure criteria conditions that would make the output wrong — before generation begins
· Use rubrics as prompt components, not just post-hoc grading tools
· Build source-check and calculation-check requirements into the specification
· Ask "what would make this wrong?" as a prompt design habit
data.section: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: THE CORE IDEA: CRITERIA-FIRST SPECIFICATION
blocks: In software engineering, test-driven development is a practice where programmers write the tests before writing the code
The Core Idea: Criteria-First Specificat — Criteria-first prompting applies the same logic to Claude. You write the criteria the output must meet before Claude gen

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — FOUR TYPES OF EVALUATION CRITE

data.title: FOUR TYPES OF EVALUATION CRITERIA.
blocks: Accuracy criteria
Completeness criteria
Format criteria
Failure criteria

S05.meta: // SLIDE 05 — BEFORE AND AFTER: ADDING CRITE

data.title: BEFORE AND AFTER: ADDING CRITERIA TO A PROMPT
blocks: · Whether "one page" means 250 words or 500
· Which findings the commissioning agency considers primary
· Whether conclusions should be stated or whether the summary should remain descriptive
· How to handle findings that the report qualifies or disputes
· Whether the format matches the agency's template

data.title: RUBRICS AS PROMPT COMPONENTS.
blocks: — Claim support
— Concision
— Audience

S08.meta: // SLIDE 08 — CRITERIA-FIRST AS A WRITER'S P

data.title: CRITERIA-FIRST AS A WRITER'S PRACTICE.
blocks: There is a benefit to criteria-first prompting beyond better Claude outputs: it makes you a clearer specifier. Writing c
Criteria-First as a Writer's Practice — Grant writers who write acceptance criteria before drafting learn something about what the funder requires. Analysts who

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE.
blocks: Run the accuracy checks is every figure traceable?
Run the completeness checks is every required section present?
Run the tone check does the output hold the specified register throughout?
Run the failure-condition check does the output misrepresent any qualified finding as definitive?

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Stating criteria only after the output disappoints you.
Writing criteria that are not checkable.
Omitting failure criteria.
Treating rubrics as substitutes for substantive knowledge.
Writing criteria at the end of a long prompt.
